content.text: Let me start by reading the file to understand its current state.

Now let me analyze each fix against the current file state:

**Fix 1 (line 30, `pf-v5-c-content`):** Line 30 still has `.pf-v5-c-content` — this class was removed with no v6 equivalent. Need to remove this rule block (lines 30-32).

**Fix 2 (lines 15, 16, 26, 35, 39, 43, `pf-v5-c-expandable-section__toggle*`):** Looking at the current file:
- Line 15: Already migrated to `.pf-v6-c-expandable-section__toggle-text` — but the rule says this class doesn't exist in v6 either. Wait, let me re-read the fix. Fix 2 says `pf-v5-c-expandable-section__toggle` was removed and `pf-v6-c-expandable-section__toggle` does NOT exist. But the file already has `pf-v6-` prefixed versions. The rules say these v6 classes don't exist either.

Let me re-read more carefully. The fixes say:
- Fix 2: `pf-v5-c-expandable-section__toggle` removed, no `pf-v6` equivalent
- Fix 3: `pf-v5-c-expandable-section__toggle-text` removed, no `pf-v6` equivalent  
- Fix 5: Same as Fix 2 (duplicate rule, different rule ID)
- Fix 6: Same as Fix 3 (duplicate rule, different rule ID)

The code contexts in the fixes show `pf-v5-` prefixes, but the actual file already has `pf-v6-` prefixes. This means a previous pass already did the prefix swap. But the rules explicitly state that a simple prefix swap does NOT produce valid classes — these classes were removed entirely, not renamed.

So I need to:
1. Remove the `.pf-v5-c-content { color: inherit; }` block (lines 30-32) — class removed entirely
2. Remove or replace all `pf-v6-c-expandable-section__toggle-text` references — the v6 class doesn't exist
3. Remove or replace all `pf-v6-c-expandable-section__toggle` references — the v6 class doesn't exist  
4. Remove or replace `pf-v6-c-expandable-section__toggle-icon` reference — same family, removed

However, these CSS rules are applying custom styling overrides to PatternFly components. The expandable section component still exists in PF v6, but its internal class structure changed. The styling intent (text alignment, cursor, colors) should be preserved using the new PF v6 class names or custom approaches.

Let me check what the actual PF v6 expandable section classes are:

The PF v6 expandable section uses a different internal structure. The `__toggle`, `__toggle-text`, and `__toggle-icon` sub-elements were removed/restructured. Since these classes don't exist in v6, the CSS selectors targeting them are dead code — they won't match anything.

The safest approach is to remove these dead selectors and their rule blocks, since they target non-existent classes and will have no effect. The styling intent would need to be achieved through PF component props or different selectors, but that's beyond what we can determine from the CSS alone.

Let me also check the CSS variable references to make sure I handle those properly. Line 16 has `--pf-v6-c-expandable-section__toggle-text--MarginLeft` — if the class doesn't exist, this variable also doesn't exist in v6.

Now let me write the cleaned-up file:
Now let me verify the result:
The file looks correct. Let me verify there are no remaining references to any of the removed classes:

arguments.command: grep -n 'pf-v5\|__toggle-text\|__toggle-icon\|__toggle[^-]' /home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260604T185102/forklift-console-plugin/source/src/onlineHelp/learningExperience/LearningExperiencePanel.scss || echo "No matches found — all removed classes are gone"

content.text:
All removed classes have been cleaned up. The file is correct.

## Changes Applied

### Fix 1 — `sd-css-dead-class-pf-v5-c-content` (line 30)
**Applied.** Removed the `.pf-v5-c-content { color: inherit; }` block (old lines 30–32). This class was removed in PF v6 with no equivalent.

### Fix 2 — `sd-css-dead-class-pf-v5-c-expandable-section-toggle` (lines 15, 16, 26, 35, 39, 43)
**Applied.** A previous pass had already swapped `pf-v5-` → `pf-v6-` prefixes, but the `__toggle`, `__toggle-text`, and `__toggle-icon` sub-element classes don't exist in PF v6. Removed all dead selectors:
- Removed `.pf-v6-c-expandable-section__toggle-text` block with its `--MarginLeft` variable override and `text-align: left` (old lines 15–18)
- Removed `.pf-v6-c-expandable-section__toggle { align-items: baseline; }` block (old lines 26–28)
- Replaced the three separate blocks inside `&.m-non-expandable` (toggle-text color, toggle-icon transparency, toggle cursor) with a single `.pf-v6-c-expandable-section` block that preserves the essential `cursor: default` and `color` overrides via the component's top-level class

### Fix 3 — `sd-css-dead-class-pf-v5-c-expandable-section-toggle-text` (lines 15, 16, 35)
**Applied.** Addressed as part of Fix 2 — all `__toggle-text` selectors were removed.

### Fix 4 — `semver-css-class-removed-c-content` (line 30)
**Applied.** Same change as Fix 1 (duplicate rule with different rule ID). The `.pf-v5-c-content` block was removed.

### Fix 5 — `semver-css-class-removed-c-expandable-section-toggle` (lines 15, 16, 26, 35, 39, 43)
**Applied.** Same change as Fix 2 (duplicate rule with different rule ID). All `__toggle` sub-element selectors were removed.

### Fix 6 — `semver-css-class-removed-c-expandable-section-toggle-text` (lines 15, 16, 35)
**Applied.** Same change as Fix 3 (duplicate rule with different rule ID). All `__toggle-text` selectors were removed.
